name: Groceries Dashboard

slots.description: Grocy stock todo entity — WARNING: checking items CONSUMES product in Grocy
Sensor with product image array attribute [{id, name, image}] built from Grocy via Node-RED
Sensor exposing Grocy shopping list as structured products attribute

pm_brief:
You are generating a Home Assistant Lovelace dashboard for groceries management. The dashboard uses custom:layout-card with grid-layout as its primary structure. Navigation between tabs is driven by an input_select entity — do NOT use HA sidebar tabs or views for navigation.

REQUIRED STRUCTURE:
- Outer container: custom:layout-card (layout_type: custom:grid-layout)
- Nav bar row: custom:button-card buttons that call input_select.select_option
- Content area: conditional cards, one per nav tab state
- Tabs needed: Shopping (shows shopping list with product images), Stock (todo-list), Tasks (todo-list)

CRITICAL RULES — these are verified working patterns, do not deviate:
1. Use card_mod style on ha-card for background-image on button-cards inside auto-entities templates. NEVER use styles.card for this — it is silently ignored in auto-entities template context.
2. In any Jinja for loop, {{ must appear on the SAME LINE as {% for %}. A newline between them causes red circle errors on every generated card.
3. Every state_attr() call inside a for loop MUST use 'or []': state_attr('sensor.x', 'items') or []
4. Any scrollable area MUST have display: block !important on :host. Without it, overflow rules are ignored and the scrollbar escapes to device level.
5. todo-list input field styles require shadow DOM scoping: ha-textfield: $: | — styles at .: level do not reach the input.
6. Grocy product images use ingress path URLs. Direct /api/files/ URLs return 401 in the HA browser — user has no Grocy session cookie.

For the shopping list with product images: use auto-entities with a Jinja template filter. Load products from {{SHOPPING_LIST_SENSOR}} attributes, look up images from {{PRODUCT_PICTURES_SENSOR}} attributes by matching product_id. Each item becomes a custom:button-card with card_mod background-image from the lookup.

Slot entities to use:
- Navigation: {{NAV_ENTITY}}
- Shopping list sensor: {{SHOPPING_LIST_SENSOR}}
- Product pictures sensor: {{PRODUCT_PICTURES_SENSOR}}
- Stock todo: {{STOCK_TODO}}
- Tasks todo: {{TASKS_TODO}}
review_criteria: Nav buttons call input_select.select_option service, not navigate action
Every conditional card checks state of {{NAV_ENTITY}} — not hardcoded string values
Shopping list uses auto-entities with Jinja template filter — NOT filter.include with entity patterns
In the Jinja template, {{ appears on the SAME LINE as {% for %} with no newline between them
Every state_attr() inside a for loop has 'or []' appended
Product image background uses card_mod: {style: 'ha-card { background-image: url(...) }'} — NOT styles.card
No raw markdown syntax (**, ##, ---) appears anywhere in YAML output
No code fences (```) appear anywhere in the YAML
All conditional cards have a matching 'state' value that matches a real option of {{NAV_ENTITY}}
Scrollable containers have ':host { display: block !important; overflow-y: scroll !important; }'
todo-list cards use 'entity:' pointing to correct todo entity slot
No entity IDs are guessed or fabricated — all use slot placeholders {{SLOT_NAME}} or confirmed entities
YAML is valid — no duplicate keys, no orphaned list items, no mismatched indentation
ceo_criteria: The output is a working groceries management dashboard if: (1) the YAML is syntactically valid and parseable, (2) navigation tabs are driven by {{NAV_ENTITY}} via conditional cards, (3) the shopping list view shows product images from the Grocy picture lookup sensor, (4) stock and tasks use native todo-list cards, (5) no banned patterns are present (styles.card for background-image, for loop newline, missing or [] guard, missing display:block). A dashboard that renders without red circles and scrolls within card boundaries on a 1200px screen passes. A dashboard that rebuilds navigation via HA views instead of input_select, or uses fabricated entity IDs, fails.
